div1.entryFree: Sunday, the Lord's day: sigh away --s, Ado I, 1, 204. Shr. II, 299. Shr. II, 299 Shr. II, 299 Shr. II, 299 Shr. II, 299 Shr. II, 299 Troil. I, 1, 79. Hml. I, 1, 76. velvet-guards and S. citizens, H4A III, 1, 261 (citizens apparelled in their sunday finery). there's rue for you, and here's some for me: we may call it herb-grace o' Sundays, Hml. IV, 5, 182 (when we mean to speak with elegance).
